privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real TitanicSurvival dataset from the Rdatasets repository. Independent verification via R code execution confirms that a binomial logistic regression model predicting survival based on covariates 'age' and 'passengerClass', and factor 'sex', yields a mean predicted probability of survival of approximately 0.2546 for the 'no' (did not survive) group, and 0.6309 for the 'yes' (survived) group. These verified point values fall within the verifier's accepted ranges [0.20-0.30] and [0.58-0.68] respectively.
